preamble: The shift from the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] to the voiced bilabial stop [b] is a form of fortition (strengthening). This process involves moving from a sonorous glide to a full oral occlusion, typically driven by a preference for stronger consonant onsets in specific word positions.

examples.note: Germanic [w] in loanwords often fortified to [ɡw] or [b] in various Romance varieties.
Fortition of initial glides into voiced stops to strengthen syllable onsets.

sources: Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
Lynch, J. (1998). Pacific Languages: An Introduction.
